judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with solid, well-cited evidence; Report 2 is slightly more rigorous (uses git blame, checks test coverage explicitly, flags additional risks like the bool("false")==True pitfall and settings-null AttributeError), but both fully satisfy the stated expectations.

report_1.evidence: Line 11 checks whether the key is missing (a clinic that never opened the setting has no "auto_send_reminders" key in its tenant.settings JSONB, so .get() returns None); line 12 returns True in that case.

report_2.evidence: the is None branch at line 11 is taken, and line 12 returns True. That return value is consumed directly in backend/app/jobs/reminders.py:25 — if auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant): — which gates the SMS/email send loop
Where I'd change it: backend/app/feature_flags.py:12 — change return True to return False inside auto_send_reminders_enabled (lines 10–13 as a whole is the function; line 12 is the specific line).

report_1.evidence: `compact_calendar_view_enabled` (the resolver this was copied from) gates a purely cosmetic UI preference — grandfathering existing tenants to "on" is low-risk and reversible... `auto_send_reminders_enabled` is a different risk category entirely: it triggers unsolicited, automated outbound SMS and email to patients

report_2.evidence: auto_send_reminders_enabled (added SLOT-214) is structurally identical to compact_calendar_view_enabled (added SLOT-190, feature_flags.py:4-7), which also defaults unset to True. Defaulting a calendar-density UI preference to "on" is low-stakes and fully reversible. Defaulting an automated SMS/email-to-patients feature to "on" is not

report_1.evidence: Add an env-level master switch AND'd with the per-tenant value. Right now `reminders.py`'s `run()` has no global gate at all — a grep across the whole project for anything master/kill-switch/environment-related turned up nothing wired to this flag

report_2.evidence: There is no rollout or kill-switch mechanism anywhere in this repo. My grep for feature-flag/rollout/env-based gating found nothing beyond this one per-tenant JSONB value — no cohort rollout, no global environment gate... Before flipping this on for real clinics, I'd want at least a global kill switch independent of per-tenant settings
